{"query":"Lil Tay Sophie Rain OnlyFans earnings dispute total amount claimed since launch verification","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1173,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.complex.com/pop-culture/a/kris-seavers/sophie-rain-doesnt-believe-lil-tay-made-15-million-two-weeks-onlyfans","title":"Sophie Rain Doesn't Believe Lil Tay Made $15 Million in Two Weeks on OnlyFans","content":"Rain previously accused Tay of manufacturing drama for clout.
OnlyFans star Sophie Rain thinks Lil Tay is exaggerating about making $15 million in two weeks on thr platform.
Tay, legal name is Claire Eileen Qi Hope, turned 18 over the summer and immediately launched her OnlyFans account, claiming that she made $1 million in the first three hours.
Last week, Tay posted on Instagram that her recent earnings totaled $15 million and claimed that she had raked in the sum in only two weeks, making her the “No.
Rain, 20, immediately doubted the $15 million claim, telling TMZ she found it hard to believe the former rapper could have made that much in two weeks.
Sophie, who initially said she was “proud” of the content creator two years her junior, has since shut down Tay on what she sees as exaggerated claims of success.
“Me coming out of retirement after hearing someone is claiming to make more than me,” Sophie said on TikTok after Tay challenged her to a boxing match with $60 million on the line."},{"url":"https://www.aol.com/lil-tay-claims-she-made-070644675.html","title":"Lil Tay Claims She Made $1 Million on OnlyFans Just Hours After Turning 18 - AOL","content":"Just days after turning 18, internet star Lil Tay says she earned over $1 million within her first three hours on OnlyFans — a claim that, if true, would mark a record-breaking debut.
On Sunday, August 3, the viral teen influencer, real name Claire Hope, shared a screenshot of what she says is her earnings from the adult content platform.
Proclaiming to be the "youngest girl on OnlyFans," she told her followers she made the content "one minute past midnight" on her 18th birthday.
If confirmed by the platform, Lil Tay would be dethroning a previous record held by rapper Bhad Bhabie, who claimed she made $1 million in six hours after launching her own OnlyFans in 2021.
In her post, Lil Tay included a detailed screenshot breaking down the alleged revenue from her OnlyFans debut, in which she earned $511,318.90 from subscriptions, $486,381.19 from private messages and $27,405 in tips.
Initial reactions on social media have been mixed, with some supporting the move and others questioning the ethics of the launch.
In 2023, her Instagram account posted a message falsely announcing her death, igniting a massive online firestorm.
She later resurfaced, claiming she had been hacked and was very much alive.
The influencer hinted at more content coming soon and said she turned down "$30 to $40 million" in offers from other platforms before her birthday even came.
This story was originally reported by Parade on Aug 5, 2025, where it first appeared."},{"url":"https://www.yahoo.com/entertainment/celebrity/articles/sophie-rain-calls-bs-lil-151523530.html","title":"Sophie Rain Calls BS On Lil Tay's OnlyFans Earnings Claims","content":"Sep 5, 2025 — Lil Tay claimed she made over $1 million in three hours. I always thought people typically went with lil' because it seemed simpler, but that li'l' was the technically accurate way of writing it out since the apostrophes were meant to indicate which portions of the word were cut out.","score":0.012318289,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.quora.com/For-the-short-form-of-little-is-it-lil-lil-or-lil","title":"For the short form of little, is it 'lil, li'l, or lil'?","content":"For the short form of little, is it 'lil, li'l, or lil'?","score":0.0110989455,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.dictionary.com/culture/slang/lil","title":"li'l | Slang","content":"# li’l. ## or lil or lil’. *Li’l* is an abbreviation of the word *little*. It’s often used in names or titles to emphasize youth or physical size. In American English, it’s often associated with rural or Southern communities. One of its earliest recorded appearances was in the title of the song “Li’l Liza Jane,” which dates back before 1900 as a field melody sung by enslaved African-Americans. Another use of the word was in the title of a satirical American comic called *Li’l Abner*, which was about rural Southern life. In recent years, *li’l* (often spelled *lil*) has found popularity within the hip-hop community as a prefix for rappers’ stage names (e.g., Lil Wayne and Lil’ Kim). By 2017, there were more than 50 musicians whose names begin with *li’l*, *lil*, or *lil’.*. > “But now, hip-hop is dominated by li'l people - in stature and in age.
